atoms.source_snippet: pub struct PreparedTx<PoolTx> { pub bundle: TxBundle<PoolTx>, pub effective_tip: u128, pub original_index: usize, pub estimated_gas: u64, pub estimated_size: usize }
atoms.semantic_claim: The payload execution layer introduces a prepared-transaction record that carries the original bundle, precomputed effective tip, stable input order, estimated gas, and estimated byte size before sorting or execution.

destinations.snippet: enum PayloadCandidate { FulfilledAsync { effective_tip, original_index, .. }, Delivery { effective_tip, original_index, .. }, Regular { candidate, effective_tip } } ... sort_candidates_by_effective_tip(...); reorder_payload_candidates(..., |candidate| candidate.primary_tx())
destinations.evidence: The current port collects fulfilled async, delivery, regular, and pooled scheduled candidates into one PayloadCandidate list, sorts it by descending effective tip with original-index tie-breaking, then applies sequencing-rights reordering over each candidate's primary transaction.

destinations.snippet: for obligation in scheduled_obligations { ... } ... let mut payload_candidates = Vec::new();
destinations.evidence: Scheduler-state obligations synthesized from contract state still execute before the PayloadCandidate list is built; only pooled scheduled transactions flow through the unified candidate sort.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Core ordering inputs are now structurally present in the port, but the old TxBundle/PreparedTx public abstraction is not preserved and scheduler-state obligations are not part of the candidate sort.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Construct mixed regular, scheduled, fulfilled, and delivery candidates with equal and unequal tips and assert the destination ordering/accounting does not rely on a missing prepared-record snapshot.
atoms.notes: Current PayloadCandidate covers the core prepared-record/list/sort behavior for fulfilled async, delivery, regular, and pooled scheduled candidates; scheduler-state obligations remain intentionally staged ahead of the list and per-source counters are observability-only.

atoms.source_snippet: let effective_tip = bundle.effective_tip_per_gas(base_fee).unwrap_or(0); let estimated_gas = bundle.estimated_gas(); let estimated_size = bundle.total_size();
atoms.semantic_claim: PreparedTx::new snapshots effective tip first, defaulting invalid fee math to zero, then snapshots estimated gas and total encoded size from the bundle.

atoms.source_snippet: test_execution_result_default asserts cumulative_gas_used == 0 and total_fees == U256::ZERO for ExecutionResult::default()
atoms.semantic_claim: A unit test pins the default unified execution result to zero gas and zero fees.

destinations.snippet: No ExecutionResult struct or test_execution_result_default equivalent exists in the destination payload files.
destinations.evidence: Searches found no ExecutionResult::default payload test in the port.

atoms.notes: Confirmed support/test-only absence: the old default UnifiedExecutionResult test is tied to an absent helper struct and does not indicate a runtime FSM gap.

atoms.semantic_claim: Sequencing-rights application is a no-op for an empty prepared collection and exits before state access, classification, metrics, or logging.

destinations.snippet: let classification = try_classify_static(tx).unwrap_or_else(|| classify_top_level_with_sr(tx, &reader, state.as_ref())); ... reorder_block(classified, parent_header, client)
destinations.evidence: Payload sequencing-rights classification is wired for static and top-level classification over PayloadCandidate::primary_tx and fails open to the existing order on state/read/reorder errors.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination extends the early no-op to single-candidate regular pools, which is equivalent for ordering because one item cannot be reordered and avoids unnecessary state reads.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Call the destination sequencing helper with an empty candidate list and a state provider that would fail if accessed; assert no state read occurs and the output remains empty.
atoms.notes: Confirmed covered for static/top-level sequencing-rights classification and fail-open reorder behavior in payload-local sequencing_rights.rs; no further review required.

atoms.source_snippet: match state_provider.state_by_block_hash(parent_header.hash_slow()) { Ok(s) => s, Err(e) => { warn!(...); return; } }
atoms.semantic_claim: If parent-state lookup fails during sequencing-rights classification, payload construction preserves the original order and logs a warning instead of failing the build.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Both paths are fail-open for builder liveness and preserve pre-sequencing order when parent state is unavailable; the destination behavior applies to regular candidates only.

atoms.source_snippet: try_classify_static(tx) returns immediately; if !use_full_call_graph then classify_top_level_with_sr(tx, &reader, &state)
atoms.semantic_claim: Sequencing classification first uses static no-interaction classification, then defaults to top-level target and selector classification unless full-call-graph mode is enabled.

destinations.snippet: let classification = try_classify_static(tx).unwrap_or_else(|| classify_top_level_with_sr(tx, &reader, state.as_ref())); ... reorder_block(classified, parent_header, client)

atoms.equivalence_argument: For regular transactions under the default no-full-call-graph mode, destination classification follows the same branch order and helper semantics as the source.

atoms.source_snippet: let use_full_call_graph = sequencing_rights_full_call_graph_enabled(); ... ContractCallInspector::new(); StateProviderDatabase::new(&state); State::builder().with_database(state_db).with_bundle_update().build(); evm_config.evm_env(parent_header); evm_config.evm_with_env_and_inspector(...); evm.transact(tx.clone());
atoms.semantic_claim: When the sequencing-rights full-call-graph environment flag is enabled, payload classification simulates each transaction with ContractCallInspector over parent state to classify internal contract calls before reordering.

destinations.snippet: pub fn sequencing_rights_full_call_graph_enabled() -> bool { ... } ... pub struct ContractCallInspector { pub contract_calls: HashMap<Address, SelectorSet>, pub call_order: Vec<(Address, Selector)>, pub depth: u32 }
destinations.evidence: The dependency contains the flag parser and inspector, but searches found no production payload call to ContractCallInspector::new, sequencing_rights_full_call_graph_enabled, or into_classification_with_sr.

destinations.snippet: let classification = try_classify_static(&candidate.tx).unwrap_or_else(|| { classify_top_level_with_sr(&candidate.tx, &reader, state.as_ref()) });
destinations.evidence: The destination payload reorder always uses top-level classification after the static fast path and never constructs an EVM for call-graph inspection.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Enable RITUAL_SEQUENCING_RIGHTS_FULL_CALL_GRAPH_ENABLED and send a transaction whose top-level target lacks sequencing rights but internally calls an SR contract; assert the builder and verifier either both reorder/reject by the internal call or both deliberately ignore it.
atoms.notes: Confirmed missing: ContractCallInspector/full-call-graph sequencing-rights classification is not wired into the payload builder; only static/top-level classification is used.

atoms.semantic_claim: Full-call-graph simulation success filters inspector results to contracts with sequencing rights; simulation failure logs and classifies the transaction as having no sequencing rights, preserving builder liveness.

destinations.snippet: let sr_contracts: Vec<_> = self.contract_calls.iter().filter(|(addr, _)| reader.has_sequencing_rights(**addr, state)).map(|(addr, selectors)| (*addr, selectors.clone())).collect(); match sr_contracts.len() { 0 => NoSequencingRights, 1 => SingleContract(...), _ => MultiContract }
destinations.evidence: The dependency implements the SR-filtered success classification, but no destination production path calls it.

destinations.snippet: classify_top_level_with_sr(&candidate.tx, &reader, state.as_ref())
destinations.evidence: No destination payload branch handles EVM simulation errors by converting them to NoSequencingRights because no simulation branch exists.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Enable full-call-graph mode with a transaction that reverts during simulation after an internal SR call and assert the destination fail-open/fail-closed behavior is explicit and matches verifier policy.
atoms.notes: Confirmed missing: ContractCallInspector/full-call-graph sequencing-rights classification is not wired into the payload builder; only static/top-level classification is used.

atoms.source_snippet: let reordered = match reorder_block(classified_txs, parent_header, state_provider) { Ok(txs) => txs, Err(e) => { warn!(...); return; } };
atoms.semantic_claim: Sequencing-rights reorder errors are fail-open in the builder: the function logs the error and leaves the existing transaction order intact.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The source explicitly compares lengths before sorting; the destination encodes the same safety property in permutation construction, because a length mismatch prevents mutation.

atoms.source_snippet: let hash_to_new_pos = reordered.iter().enumerate().map(|(pos, tx)| (*tx.tx_hash(), pos)).collect(); collection.transactions.sort_by_key(|pt| hash_to_new_pos.get(pt.bundle.primary_tx().tx_hash()).copied().unwrap_or(usize::MAX));
atoms.semantic_claim: After successful sequencing-rights reorder, the prepared collection is reordered by primary transaction hash, with unmapped hashes sorted to the end.

atoms.source_snippet: ritual_metrics::block::log_payload_phase("payload_sequencing_rights", block_number, ts_start, ts_end, collection.transactions.len()); debug!(tx_count = collection.transactions.len(), "Applied sequencing rights reordering with EVM simulation");
atoms.semantic_claim: Successful sequencing-rights application records a payload phase metric with timing and transaction count, then emits a debug log.

destinations.snippet: debug!(target: "ritual::sequencing_rights", tx_count = candidates.len(), "applied sequencing-rights order to regular pool candidates");
destinations.evidence: The destination preserves a debug log on successful regular candidate reordering but does not call ritual_metrics::block::log_payload_phase for payload_sequencing_rights.

atoms.notes: Confirmed observability gap only: the old payload phase metric for sequencing-rights timing/counts is absent, while the behavioral reorder path is present.

summary: Mechanical review complete. Current payload code now covers the old prepared-collection core through PayloadCandidate: fulfilled async, delivery, regular, and pooled scheduled candidates share effective-tip sorting, original-index tie-breaking, and payload-level sequencing-rights reordering. Remaining confirmed gaps are narrower: scheduler-state obligations still execute before the candidate list, optional full-call-graph sequencing classification is not wired, and the old per-source counters/phase metrics are absent.
notes: Reviewed against current port after PayloadCandidate landed; no atoms remain in needs-review state.
